title: Systems and methods for domain-specific machine-interpretation of input data
abstract: In an embodiment receiving input data over a computer network. The method also includes automatically generating topic-based metadata of the input data in relation to a multi-level taxonomy. The method also includes automatically determining parts of speech of tokens of the input data. The method also includes searching a metadata store using the tokens and the parts of speech, the searching including identifying, at a first level and a second level of the multi-level taxonomy, nodes that include metadata which sufficiently match the tokens and the parts of speech of the tokens, where the searching yields best-match grammar-based metadata. The method of also includes revising the best-match grammar-based metadata based at least in part on the automatically generated topic -based metadata.
claims: Claims CLAIMS  What is claimed is: 1. A method of machine-interpreting freeform text, the method comprising, by a computer system: receiving input data over a computer network; automatically generating topic -based metadata of the input data in relation to a multilevel taxonomy; automatically determining parts of speech of tokens of the input data; searching a metadata store using the tokens and the parts of speech, the searching comprising identifying, at a first level and a second level of the multi-level taxonomy, nodes that include metadata which sufficiently match the tokens and the parts of speech of the tokens, wherein the searching yields best-match grammar-based metadata; and revising the best-match grammar-based metadata based at least in part on the automatically generated topic-based metadata. 2. The method of claim 1, wherein the automatically generating topics comprises: projecting the input data on a vector space based on a pre-trained model, the projecting yielding an inferred vector; identifying vectors of the pre-trained model that are deemed sufficiently similar to the inferred vector; accessing in-memory pre-associated topics of the identified vectors; processing the in-memory pre-associated topics, the processing the in-memory pre- associated topics yielding a de-duplicated topic list in relation to topic -relevancy measures; accessing in-memory pre-associated metadata descriptors of the de-duplicated topics, wherein the in-memory pre-associated metadata descriptors correspond to nodes of the multilevel taxonomy; and processing the in-memory pre-associated metadata descriptors, the processing the in- memory pre-associated metadata descriptors yielding a de-duplicated descriptor list in relation to descriptor-relevancy measures. 3. The method of claim 1, comprising: parsing the input data to yield a segment collection comprising domain-relevant textual segments in relation to domain-relevance values, wherein the parsing comprises dividing the input data into textual segments; and removing, from the divided input data, content deemed non-relevant to a particular domain, wherein the removing yields the domain-relevant textual segments; and wherein the automatically generating topics and the automatically determining parts of speech are performed on the parsed input data. 4. The method of claim 3, comprising, prior to the automatically determining parts of speech: measuring domain relevance of the domain-relevant textual segments to the particular domain, wherein the measuring domain relevance yields the domain-relevance values; and verifying a threshold domain relevance of the parsed input data as a precondition of executing the automatically determining parts of speech, wherein the verifying is based at least in part on the domain-relevance values. 5. The method of claim 4, the method comprising: prior to the searching, adjusting the segment collection in a rule-based fashion; and wherein the searching is performed using the adjusted segment collection. 6. The method of claim 5, the adjusting comprising: recognizing a compound statement in a particular textual segment of the textual segments; transforming the compound statement into two or more separate segments; and causing the two or more separate segments to replace the particular textual segment in the segment collection. 7. The method of claim 5, the adjusting comprising removing a particular textual segment from the segment collection based at least in part on a result of the automatically determining parts of speech. 8. The method of claim 5, wherein the adjusting comprises removing a textual segment from the segment collection. 9. The method of claim 5, wherein the adjusting comprises removing a portion of a textual segment in the segment collection. 10. The method of claim 1, wherein the revising the best-match grammar-based metadata comprises boosting relevancy measures of particular grammar-based metadata based, at least in part, on a determination that the particular grammar-based metadata and particular topic- based metadata correspond to a same node of the multi-level taxonomy. 11. The method of claim 1, wherein the revising the best-match grammar-based metadata comprises boosting relevancy measures of particular grammar-based metadata based, at least in part, on a determination that the particular grammar-based metadata corresponds to a child node of particular topic-based metadata in the multi-level taxonomy. 12. A computer system comprising a processor and memory, wherein the processor and memory in combination are operable to perform a method comprising: receiving input data over a computer network; automatically generating topic -based metadata of the input data in relation to a multilevel taxonomy; automatically determining parts of speech of tokens of the input data; searching a metadata store using the tokens and the parts of speech, the searching comprising identifying, at a first level and a second level of the multi-level taxonomy, nodes that include metadata which sufficiently match the tokens and the parts of speech of the tokens, wherein the searching yields best-match grammar-based metadata; and revising the best-match grammar-based metadata based at least in part on the automatically generated topic-based metadata. 13. The computer system of claim 12, wherein the automatically generating topics comprises: projecting the input data on a vector space based on a pre-trained model, the projecting yielding an inferred vector; identifying vectors of the pre-trained model that are deemed sufficiently similar to the inferred vector; accessing in-memory pre-associated topics of the identified vectors; processing the in-memory pre-associated topics, the processing the in-memory pre- associated topics yielding a de-duplicated topic list in relation to topic -relevancy measures; accessing in-memory pre-associated metadata descriptors of the de-duplicated topics, wherein the in-memory pre-associated metadata descriptors correspond to nodes of the multilevel taxonomy; and processing the in-memory pre-associated metadata descriptors, the processing the in- memory pre-associated metadata descriptors yielding a de-duplicated descriptor list in relation to descriptor-relevancy measures. 14. The computer system of claim 12, the method comprising: parsing the input data to yield a segment collection comprising domain-relevant textual segments in relation to domain-relevance values, wherein the parsing comprises dividing the input data into textual segments; and removing, from the divided input data, content deemed non-relevant to a particular domain, wherein the removing yields the domain-relevant textual segments; and wherein the automatically generating topics and the automatically determining parts of speech are performed on the parsed input data. 15. The computer system of claim 14, the method comprising, prior to the automatically determining parts of speech: measuring domain relevance of the domain-relevant textual segments to the particular domain, wherein the measuring domain relevance yields the domain-relevance values; and verifying a threshold domain relevance of the parsed input data as a precondition of executing the automatically determining parts of speech, wherein the verifying is based at least in part on the domain-relevance values. 16. The computer system of claim 15, the method comprising: prior to the searching, adjusting the segment collection in a rule-based fashion; and wherein the searching is performed using the adjusted segment collection. 17. The computer system of claim 16, the adjusting comprising: recognizing a compound statement in a particular textual segment of the textual segments; transforming the compound statement into two or more separate segments; and causing the two or more separate segments to replace the particular textual segment in the segment collection. 18. The computer system of claim 16, the adjusting comprising removing a particular textual segment from the segment collection based at least in part on a result of the automatically determining parts of speech. 19. The computer system of claim 12, wherein the revising the best-match grammar- based metadata comprises boosting relevancy measures of particular grammar-based metadata based, at least in part, on a determination that the particular grammar-based metadata and particular topic-based metadata correspond to a same node of the multi-level taxonomy. 20. A computer-program product comprising a non-transitory computer-usable medium having computer-readable program code embodied therein, the computer-readable program code adapted to be executed to implement a method comprising: receiving input data over a computer network; automatically generating topic -based metadata of the input data in relation to a multilevel taxonomy; automatically determining parts of speech of tokens of the input data; searching a metadata store using the tokens and the parts of speech, the searching comprising identifying, at a first level and a second level of the multi-level taxonomy, nodes that include metadata which sufficiently match the tokens and the parts of speech of the tokens, wherein the searching yields best-match grammar-based metadata; and revising the best-match grammar-based metadata based at least in part on the automatically generated topic-based metadata.
